situation: Coldplay Music of the Spheres concert at Bukit Jalil National Stadium. Saturday evening, clear weather. Estimated 80,000 attendance.

outcome.platform_incidents: minor crowding at Bukit Jalil station during 9pm exodus
outcome.verdict: Successful. 14 trains/hr was sufficient. Could have managed with 12 during first half, but surge at concert end justified full deployment throughout.

situation: Hari Raya Aidilfitri eve. Wednesday evening. Clear weather. Mass outstation travel, unusually high ridership across all lines.

outcome.platform_incidents: significant queuing at Masjid Jamek interchange — bottleneck point
outcome.verdict: Insufficient. 12 trains/hr caused overflow at interchange stations. Should have deployed 15 trains/hr from 4pm onwards. Key lesson: holiday eve traffic peaks earlier than expected (4pm not 5pm).

situation: Merdeka Day national celebration at Dataran Merdeka. Saturday. Cloudy. 60,000 expected at parade, heavy pedestrian flow toward city centre.

outcome.verdict: Well managed. 10 trains/hr kept load factor at comfortable 78%. Conservative approach worked because crowd dispersal was gradual (not a single post-event exodus).

situation: New Year's Eve countdown event at KLCC and Dataran Merdeka. Tuesday night, 9pm to 1am. Clear weather.

outcome.platform_incidents: severe overcrowding at KL Sentral and Dang Wangi — passengers waited 3+ trains to board. Complaints filed.
outcome.verdict: Failed. 8 trains/hr severely inadequate for NYE demand. Should have deployed maximum 20 trains/hr. Lesson: late-night events require aggressive frequency even if cost is high, as stranded passengers create safety and reputational risk.

situation: Malaysia Cup football final at Bukit Jalil. Saturday afternoon. Heavy rain. 50,000 attendance.

outcome.platform_incidents: moderate crowding — rain pushed more fans onto LRT instead of driving
outcome.verdict: Adequate but tight. Rain increased LRT ridership beyond prediction — rainy weather consistently adds 15-20% more passengers than forecast. Recommend adding 2 extra trains on top of model prediction whenever weather is rainy for outdoor events.

situation: Normal Friday morning peak hour, no events. Clear weather.

outcome.verdict: Standard operation. 15 trains/hr maintains comfortable 72% load factor during peak. This is the validated baseline for weekday peak hours.

situation: Signal failure on Ampang line caused passenger rerouting to Sri Petaling. Sunday afternoon. Clear weather.

outcome.platform_incidents: queuing at Sri Petaling and Bukit Jalil stations for ~45 minutes until Ampang line restored
outcome.verdict: Marginal. 9 trains/hr nearly reached capacity. Should have immediately deployed 12 trains/hr upon receiving emergency alert. Key lesson: emergency rerouting adds demand faster than standard event surges — act fast.

situation: Chinese New Year eve. Thursday evening. Clear weather. High outstation travel volume, many workers heading home early.

outcome.verdict: Comfortable. 13 trains/hr was slightly more than needed — could have used 11. However, the conservative choice was justified given uncertainty around exact travel timing during festive periods.
